generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components //
instance PermafrostTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder deployed on the first vertical robot axis to measure real-time positional data with high precision for automated one-way data flow to the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance TorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Torque sensor integrated with the permanent magnet synchronous motor to capture dynamic load variations for friction analysis in the manufacturing automation system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TorqueDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance PositionEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PermafrostTemperatureSensor
]

instance TorqueDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TorqueSensor
]

instance GearboxDynamicDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TorqueSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram representing the component-connector architecture of the first vertical robot axis, including motor, gear transmission, and cycloidal gear components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolchainEnabler, MontiArcEnabler
]

instance AADLComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AADL component-based model defining modular system structure for the robot axis with interfaces and connectors for behavioral validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AADLToolchainEnabler
]

instance SimulinkDynamicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model for detailed behavioral simulation of dynamic system interactions, including friction modeling and control logic validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance MontiArcAutomatonModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc state machine model representing behavioral dynamics of the robot axis components for friction analysis and failure scenario simulation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcEnabler
]

instance PositionalDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and abstracted positional data traces from the physical system's encoders, enabling real-time monitoring of motion accuracy and friction identification."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PositionEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance TorqueDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured torque data traces derived from sensor measurements, abstracted for downstream friction analysis and performance optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TorqueDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance GearboxDynamicDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Contextual abstraction of raw sensor data combining positional and torque measurements for comprehensive gearbox dynamics analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxDynamicDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLToolchainEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MBSE platform enabling integrated modeling across SysML and Simulink tools for multi-abstraction modeling and bidirectional data flow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalShadowMonitoringService, FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance AADLToolchainEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AADL-based toolchain supporting component-based modeling and interface definition for the robot axis system architecture."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComponentValidationService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink simulation engine for detailed behavioral modeling and dynamic system analysis of the robot axis components."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationDrivenTestingService
]

instance MontiArcEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc framework enabling state-based behavioral modeling and automaton simulation for system dynamics validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralValidationService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software module aggregating and abstracting raw sensor data traces into structured digital representations for downstream analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance MQTTProtocolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation enabling real-time or near-real-time streaming of sensor data from physical system to digital shadow representation."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataStreamingService
]

instance RealTimeDataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling real-time streaming of sensor data from physical system to digital shadow representation."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalDashboardInsight
]

// C6: Services
instance DigitalShadowMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Passive monitoring service aggregating and abstracting contextual data traces from the physical robot axis for friction identification without autonomous control."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionIdentificationInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Contextual abstraction service converting raw sensor measurements into structured digital traces for diagnostic purposes by heterogeneous stakeholders."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizationInsight
]

instance ComponentValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Component-based validation service using AADL models to ensure modular system structure integrity and interface compliance."
    DTDFVocab:provides StructuralValidationInsight
]

instance SimulationDrivenTestingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation-based testing service validating virtual prototypes through stress tests and failure scenario simulations using Simulink models."
    DTDFVocab:provides RobustnessValidationInsight
]

instance BehavioralValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Behavioral validation service using MontiArc automaton models to simulate dynamic interactions and validate system behavior under various conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides DynamicBehaviorInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Visualization service presenting aggregated data traces through graphical dashboards for operational teams and maintenance personnel."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalDashboardInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionIdentificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight generated from passive data aggregation identifying friction points in the robot axis mechanical components through contextual data traces."
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight derived from contextual abstraction enabling performance optimization recommendations based on aggregated sensor data analysis."
]

instance StructuralValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation insight confirming the structural integrity and interface compliance of the robot axis components through AADL model analysis."
]

instance RobustnessValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight from simulation-driven testing validating the system's robustness through stress tests and failure scenario simulations."
]

instance DynamicBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Behavioral insight from MontiArc automaton simulations showing system dynamics and validating operational behavior under various conditions."
]

instance OperationalDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of aggregated data traces through graphical dashboards enabling operational monitoring and maintenance decision-making."
]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Precision manufacturing environment where the serial robot performs high-precision tasks requiring real-time monitoring of mechanical friction and positional accuracy."
    base:contains MABIRobotAxisComponent, PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotorComponent, GearTransmissionSystemComponent
]

// C3: Physical Components
instance MABIRobotAxisComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "First vertical robot axis serving as the primary structural element for motion control in the MABI MAX 100 serial robot system."

]

instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotorComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "High-torque efficiency PMSM driving mechanism integrated with the robot axis for precise motion control in manufacturing automation."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment
]

instance GearTransmissionSystemComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Mechanical power conversion system including cycloidal gear for high-precision reduction and smooth motion control in the robot axis."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment
]

// C5: Digital Components
instance DigitalShadowRepresentation : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Passive digital representation aggregating and abstracting real-time sensor data from the physical robot axis for friction analysis without bidirectional control."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment
]

instance BehavioralModelComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital component implementing behavioral models (Simulink, MontiArc) for dynamic system analysis and friction modeling validation."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment
]

instance SysMLModelComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component containing SysML models representing the functional and logical architecture of the robot axis system."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingAutomationEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the described Digital Twin system is **not explicitly detailed** in the provided documents, as the focus is primarily on **digital shadows** (unidirectional data flow) and **modeling methodologies** (e.g., MBSE, ADLs). However, the broader context implies that a Digital Twin **can** provide active components to **interpret and process** incoming data and potentially **push commands back** to physical systems [35–37].

**Inferred Components (if applicable):**
- **Control flows** would likely involve **command validation**, **authentication**, and **feedback mechanisms** (e.g., confirmation of command execution).
- **Conflict resolution** may rely on **rule-based systems** or **simulation-based validation** (e.g., using **SysML state machines** [17] or **MontiArc Automaton** [25]).
- **Feedback systems** could include **real-time monitoring** of physical responses (e.g., via MQTT [52] or IoT platforms [53]) to adjust virtual models dynamically.

**Technical Gaps:**
- **No explicit mention** of command types (e.g., REST APIs, OPC UA), message formats (e.g., JSON, XML), or **authentication protocols** (e.g., OAuth, TLS) in the documents.
- **No quantification** of latency, reliability, or **command execution confirmation** mechanisms.

**Conclusion:**
Since the documents **do not provide specific details** on bidirectional control flows, this characteristic is **not explicitly supported** by the provided context. For a comprehensive DT system, such interactions would typically involve **IoT gateways**, **edge computing**, and **real-time middleware** (e.g., ThingsBoard [53]), but these are **not described** here.
Not in Document
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the documents operates within a **real-time, data-driven** temporal framework, where data traces are collected and processed as **automated one-way flows** from the physical system to the digital representation. The system emphasizes **fine-grained traceability** of data traces, enabling transitive tracking of their origins (e.g., processing components, input traces, or models) via **association links** (e.g., 'originates from' relationships). While the documents do not specify explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., millisecond-level latency targets or sampling intervals), the **streaming-based data collection** (implied by MQTT usage in horizontal integration) suggests low-latency requirements for maintaining temporal consistency. No explicit time scales (e.g., microsecond, millisecond, or second granularity) or orchestration of multi-timescale services (e.g., real-time control vs. predictive analytics) are detailed in the provided context."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** is orchestrated through a **reference model-based approach**, where the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** defines foundational concepts (e.g., data traces, processing components, metadata) that are **concretized** into a **conforming model** for specific use cases [32, 41]. This involves **architecture patterns** derived from **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., SysML [17], ADLs like AADL [24], or MontiArc [25, 26]), which describe systems via **component & connector perspectives** (e.g., interfaces, interconnections, composition). The constellation integrates **heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives** [31] by combining models from different notations (e.g., SysML IBDs, state machines, or simulation tools like Simulink [28]). Resource management is implied through the **processing logic** embedded in data calculation or simulation models, which operate on input data traces to produce output data. System-wide coordination is achieved via **traceability links** (e.g., transitive associations) that map data traces to their origins (processing components, models) and ensure **fine-grained traceability** [32]. No explicit mention of orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices) or resource allocation strategies is provided."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which decompose system modeling into hierarchical abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. Transition management between these stages is facilitated by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define component-connector perspectives, interfaces, and interconnections. Behavior modeling at the logical and product layers integrates **state machines (SysML v1.7), MontiArc Automaton, or external simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose languages like C++/Java). Lifecycle governance involves **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **multi-tool interoperability**, addressing complexity through modularized modeling approaches like **RFLP (Requirements-Functions-Logics-Physics), OOSEM, SPES, or SMArDT**. No explicit lifecycle governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are referenced in the document, but the emphasis on **abstraction, toolchain heterogeneity, and stakeholder alignment** implies iterative refinement phases from conceptual design to operational deployment."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity in the Digital Twin system is evaluated through **comparative analysis of simulated vs. measured data**, where deviations between model predictions and real-world sensor inputs (e.g., encoder readings) are analyzed to assess model accuracy. However, the documents do not explicitly outline **formal Verification & Validation (V&V) methodologies**, **uncertainty quantification frameworks**, or **error correction mechanisms**. Instead, fidelity is implicitly addressed via **cross-model consistency checks** required when integrating heterogeneous notations (e.g., SysML, Simulink) within the MBSE framework. Quality assurance processes are not detailed beyond the need to 'cope with models in different notations and tools'"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection for this Digital Twin system is limited to the **MQTT protocol** for streaming sensor data from physical assets to the digital shadow, as referenced in [52]. The system’s passive nature (as a digital shadow) implies no active control or feedback loops, thus no bidirectional communication protocols (e.g., REST APIs, WebSockets, or proprietary protocols) are mentioned. The only technical connection explicitly noted is the **unidirectional data flow via MQTT**, with no further elaboration on infrastructure or communication architecture."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems via **MQTT-based streaming protocols** for data exchange, as implied by the context of digital shadows in manufacturing. While the documents do not explicitly mention **OPC UA** or other protocols, the use of MQTT suggests adherence to **lightweight, publish-subscribe interoperability standards** for real-time data flow. The system references **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** and **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** within the Digital Shadow Reference Model, but these are framed as internal architectural concepts rather than horizontal integration with external enterprise systems. No details are provided on **API gateways**, **service-oriented architectures (SOA)**, or **enterprise integration patterns** (e.g., ESBs). The scope of horizontal integration is limited to the **data traces and processing components** defined for the specific use case, with no explicit dependencies on external systems beyond the streaming infrastructure."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates a **safety cell** mechanism to restrict the operational working range of robotic assets, ensuring physical safety by confining movement within predefined boundaries. Sensor data, including time series from all sensors, is transmitted via **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)**, a lightweight protocol designed for IoT and real-time data streaming. While the documents do not explicitly detail encryption protocols or authentication systems, the use of MQTT implies reliance on underlying transport security measures (e.g., TLS) for data integrity and confidentiality during transmission. No explicit mention of **RAMI 4.0 cybersecurity layers**, **blockchain audit logs**, **IEC 61508 compliance**, or **federated learning** is present in the provided context. The system’s safety considerations are primarily hardware-based (safety cell) rather than software-based protocols or regulatory frameworks."
]
